name: German Springerle Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 300°F (150°C).
2. In a large bowl, beat the eggs until very light and fluffy.
3. Gradually add the powdered sugar while continuing to beat at high speed until the mixture resembles a soft meringue. This is crucial; do not rush this step.
4. Add the anise oil and blend gently.
5. Sift together the flour and baking soda, then add them to the egg mixture in the bowl with low speed.
6. Cover the bowl tightly and let it rest for 15 minutes.
7. Divide the dough into thirds. Roll each piece into an 8-inch square, about 1/4 inch thick.
8. While working with one portion of dough, keep the others covered.
9. Dust a Springerle mold with flour and press the dough into it. Cut cookies apart.
10. Place the cookies on a lightly floured surface and let them stand overnight.
11. Preheat the baking sheet in the oven to 300°F (150°C).
12. Sprinkle anise seeds over the cookies, moisten lightly with water, and place them on the prepared baking sheets.
13. Bake for about 20 minutes and store tightly covered.
